text: Hello, this is a case of laparoscopic appendicitomy. This patient has the previous two episodes of the acute appendicitis and right now she is in little interval state.
The surgeon uses a Striker mini alligator to grasp the appendix and major appendix, while a harmonic device is employed to separate the appendix from the major appendix.
The surgeon encounters an electrocecal appendix with adhesions present between the appendix and the paracolic gutter, as well as the peritoneum of the abdominal wall.
The surgeon uses a striker mini alligator to hold the appendix securely, allowing for dissection with a single instrument. Medial traction is applied to mobilize the appendix and expose its base.
The surgeon skeletonizes the appendix, ensuring complete removal from the base to prevent stump appendicitis.
The surgeon uses an extracorporeal Melger knot technique, introducing the striker mini alligator into the loop to securely grasp the appendix.
The surgeon uses a knot pusher to guide the loop towards the base of the appendix, positioning it slightly towards the cecum to ensure the knot tightens effectively at the cecum during tying.

The surgeon uses an extracorporeal knot technique, driving the tip of the knot pusher towards the cecum to securely tighten the knot around the base of the appendix. The knot is tightened multiple times to ensure stability.
The surgeon completes the dissection and uses scissors to cut the appendix, followed by the harmonic scalpel to further divide the appendix from its base.
The surgeon uses harmonic scissors to cut the appendix, sealing the distal portion and cauterizing the mucosa to prevent future mucosal issues.
So it is just a very simple case of the laparoscopic appendectomy.

The surgeon uses a striker mini alligator through the five millimeter left iliac fossa port to perform dissection. A five millimeter telescope is then used to pull the appendix through the umbilicus, with reduced vision observed through the telescope.
The surgeon carefully pulls the appendix into the cannula to minimize exposure, using an endo bag to further reduce the risk of infection, before extracting the appendix from the abdominal cavity.
